title: By the Numbers
editorialSummary: Mathematical concepts grouped by category. False trail: Pi and Euler's Number are both constants but also appear in calculus — they sit in Famous Mathematical Constants. Induction is both a proof type and a scientific method — placed firmly in Mathematical Proof Types. 'Perfect Number' is a type of number (6, 28, 496...) and should not be confused with a constant.

meta.gptRationale: Everything is math-related, so the false trail density is high and the distinctions depend on formal terminology.
